name: get_file_path
short_description: Get the full path to the specific file, for the given part.
full_help:
get_file_path

Description: 
Get the full path to the specific file, for the given part.

Syntax: 
get_file_path  [-regexp] [-nocase] [-filter <arg>] [-of_objects <args>]
               [-part <arg>] [-partname <arg>] [-partobj <arg>] [-v <arg>]
               [-vaultFileId <arg>] [-useLegacy <arg>] [-filename <arg>]
               [-legacyFileId <arg>] [-dataLevel <arg>] [-isTimingFile <arg>]
               [-checkDeviceFilesDir <arg>] [-useOldAPIs <arg>]
               [-askGetDataFilePath <arg>]
               [-askGetMostSpecificDataLocation <arg>]
               [-askGetDataLocation_Filename <arg>]
               [-askGetDataLocation_DataLevel_Filename <arg>] [-quiet]
               [-verbose] [<patterns>]

Returns: 
string

Usage: 
  Name                                      Description
  -----------------------------------------------------
  [-regexp]                                 Patterns are full regular 
                                            expressions
  [-nocase]                                 Perform case-insensitive 
                                            matching. (valid only when 
                                            -regexp specified)
  [-filter]                                 Filter list with expression
  [-of_objects]                             Get the string of these part.
  [-part]                                   (same as -partname) partname to 
                                            use if not providing a part 
                                            object.
  [-partname]                               partname to use if not providing 
                                            a part object.
  [-partobj]                                part Tcl object
  [-v]                                      Verbose level where 
                                            0=None;1=Errors;2=Warnings;3=Info
                                            ;4=Trace;5=Verbose
                                            Default: 2=Warnings
  [-vaultFileId]                            Vault Key or Filename for looking
                                            up file location in vault's 
                                            device.manifest.
  [-useLegacy]                              Use legacy / pre-vault file 
                                            locating logic, where 
                                            0=NO_LEGACY_FILE_LOCATING, 
                                            1=GET_DATA_FILE_PATH, 
                                            2=GET_MOST_SPECIFIC_DATA_LOC, 
                                            3=GET_DATA_LOCATION, 
                                            4=GET_DATA_LOCATION_IN_DPX_COMMON
                                            Default: 0=NO_LEGACY_FILE_LOCATIN
                                            G
  [-filename]                               filename is an obsolete parameter
                                            because it is now ambiguous. It 
                                            will be used as the legacyFileId,
                                            but you may have intended it to 
                                            be used as the vaultFileId.  
                                            Please use those two parameters 
                                            instead of this one.
  [-legacyFileId]                           Legacy Filename or Token(e.g. 
                                            "<xng>".
  [-dataLevel]                              0=HDPA_ARCH, 1=HDPA_FAMILY, 
                                            2=HDPA_DEVICE, 3=HDPA_PACKAGE, 
                                            4=HDPA_SPEED to use with 
                                            getDataLocation(dataLevel, 
                                            filename) or getMostSpecificDataL
                                            ocation(dataLevel, filename, 
                                            isTimingFile(default=false), 
                                            checkDeviceFilesDir(default=false
                                            ))
                                            Default: 0=HDPA_ARCH
  [-isTimingFile]                           Legacy option to indicate a 
                                            timing file, to use with 
                                            getMostSpecificDataLocation(dataL
                                            evel, filename, isTimingFile(defa
                                            ult=false), checkDeviceFilesDir(d
                                            efault=false))
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-checkDeviceFilesDir]                    Legacy option to look in 
                                            "devicefiles" directory, to use 
                                            with getMostSpecificDataLocation(
                                            dataLevel, filename, 
                                            isTimingFile(default=false), 
                                            checkDeviceFilesDir(default=false
                                            ))
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-useOldAPIs]                             if true, force the use of 
                                            old/deprecated/legacy HDARPart 
                                            API calls directly rather then 
                                            indirectly through new HDARPart 
                                            getFilePath API.
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-askGetDataFilePath]                     ask getDataFilePath
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-askGetMostSpecificDataLocation]         ask getMostSpecificDataLocation
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-askGetDataLocation_Filename]            ask getDataLocation(filename)
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-askGetDataLocation_DataLevel_Filename]  ask getDataLocation(DataLevel, 
                                            filename)
                                            Default: 0=false
  [-quiet]                                  Ignore command errors
  [-verbose]                                Suspend message limits during 
                                            command execution
  [<patterns>]                              Match string against patterns
                                            Default: *

Categories: 
Device, Object, Internal

arguments.description: Get the string of these part.
(same as -partname) partname to use if not providing a part object.
partname to use if not providing a part object.
Vault Key or Filename for looking up file location in vault's device.manifest.

Use legacy / pre-vault file locating logic, where 0=NO_LEGACY_FILE_LOCATING, 1=GET_DATA_FILE_PATH, 2=GET_MOST_SPECIFIC_DATA_LOC, 3=GET_DATA_LOCATION, 4=GET_DATA_LOCATION_IN_DPX_COMMON Default: 0=NO_LEGACY_FILE_LOCATIN G

filename is an obsolete parameter because it is now ambiguous. It will be used as the legacyFileId, but you may have intended it to be used as the vaultFileId. Please use those two parameters instead of this one.

0=HDPA_ARCH, 1=HDPA_FAMILY, 2=HDPA_DEVICE, 3=HDPA_PACKAGE, 4=HDPA_SPEED to use with getDataLocation(dataLevel, filename) or getMostSpecificDataL ocation(dataLevel, filename, isTimingFile(default=false), checkDeviceFilesDir(default=false )) Default: 0=HDPA_ARCH
Legacy option to indicate a timing file, to use with getMostSpecificDataLocation(dataL evel, filename, isTimingFile(defa ult=false), checkDeviceFilesDir(d efault=false)) Default: 0=false
Legacy option to look in "devicefiles" directory, to use with getMostSpecificDataLocation( dataLevel, filename, isTimingFile(default=false), checkDeviceFilesDir(default=false )) Default: 0=false
if true, force the use of old/deprecated/legacy HDARPart API calls directly rather then indirectly through new HDARPart getFilePath API. Default: 0=false
Suspend message limits during command execution
